series_description: The Ohio Cancer Incidence Surveillance System (OCISS) receives cancer reports from cancer reporters (for example, hospitals, physicians, ambulatory health care providers, laboratories) either in an electronic format, on paper, or via fax in accordance with Ohio law and rules that require the reporting of this information.
retention_statement: Electronic: Five years from date of electronic receipt. At the end of each calendar year (for example, December 31, 2021), delete electronic cancer reports received more than 5 years prior (for example, on or before December 31, 2016). ; Paper: Three years after the date of diagnosis listed on paper or faxed cancer reports. At the end of each calendar year (for example, December 31, 2021), shred paper cancer reports and delete faxed cancer reports with a date of diagnosis more than 3 years prior (for example, on or before December 31, 2018).

series_description: This record series contains documents and forms pertaining to the application, survey, and certification of clinical laboritories as specified by the Clinical Laboratory Improvement Amendments of 1988 (CLIA); 42 CHR Part 493 and plasmapheresis surveys as required by ORC Chapter 3725 Plasmaphersis Centers. Documentation may include, but is not limited to, the application, correspondence, change of ownership, statement of deficiancies and plan of correction, change requests (name, address, certificate type), proficiency test summaries/reports, personnel qualifications, fees and enforcement. This schedule is applicable to all test categories (waived, moderate, and high complexity) and certificate type (waived, provider performed microscopy, accreditation, and compliance.

series_title: Plumbing and Sanitary Disposal Construction Blueprints
series_description: The Ohio Department of Health, Divisions of Sanitation and Plumbing are required by law (Section 3701.19, 3701.20 of the Ohio Revised Code) to approve construction plans for the disposal of sewage and industrial waste; and to exercise general supervision of the disposal of waste. In compliance with the above stated sections of the Ohio Revised Code, the Divisions of Plumbing and Sanitation maintain construction blueprints on all facilities, approved by the Ohio Department of Health, involving the disposal of sewage and industrial waste. In order to adequately exercise general supervision over the sanitary disposal of sewage and waste, it is necessary to periodically inspect these facilities thereby requiring the continued retention of construction blueprints. The construction blueprints and drawings are referenced frequently for a period of two years during which time, construction of the facility is ongoing, after the construction of the facility is completed, construction blueprints are needed on an infrequent basis when general facility inspections are being conducted. To adequaetely maintain the large volume of construction plans approved each year, the Ohio Department of Health intends to microfilm all approved plans and retain the microfilm in its immediate office area for two (2) years, then transfer the microfilm to the State Records Center for the duration of the approved retention period.
retention_statement: Microform: Retain until facility razed. Retain microfilm in office area until construction of facility is completed ; Paper: Microfilm immediately upon final approval of construction plans by Ohio department of Health

series_description: These forms (Coupon and Delivery Verification Receipt) contain information which allows a program participant to purchase designated food items. Each form includes the following information: Participant's name, address and signature; Vendor's name, address, and tax identification number; amount of sale; authorized food items, quantity; and redeemable dates.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain records at ODH Warehouse to verify receipt after verification, transfer to State Records Center; retain records until audited and/or three years six months, whichever is longer

series_description: (OHD 3641-09/9/75) These reports provide a statis tical base in areas of birth control, abortion rates, various demographical information about abortion patents, emdical condition of patients, types of medical procedures, and other medical information co
retention_statement: Paper: Retain until reports are "keyed in" to computer tape

series_description: Utilization data for all hospitals in the state, the plant evaluation survey and registration documentation.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain 15 years ; Paper: Retain 5 years, review by state for historical value; if none, dispose

series_description: Contains area designation requests and corresponding responses from the federal government regarding Health Manpower Shortage Areas (HMSA).
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain for 3 years ; Paper: Retain for 3 years

series_description: Pursuant to Chapter 3705 of the Revised Code, Vital Statistics accepts checks in payment of requests for certified/uncertified copies as well as birth lists and searching fees. Those checks dishonored by the bank are returned. If uncollectable by our office and the State Treasurer, they are written off as a loss.
retention_statement: Paper: Retained until audited by Auditor of State and audit is released.

series_description: Includes informaiton about laboratory equipment temperature readings, and related monitoring. Includes information about the condition of the equipment.
retention_statement: Paper: Maintain in office for three years

series_description: Contains non-confidential complaint information about residential care facilities on a form and an index card format. Used to track all of the complaints made against a facility/provider.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain for the life of the facility.

series_description: Includes information regarding adult care facility survey findings, correspondence between ODH and facility operators, license issuance/revocation and complaints.
retention_statement: Paper: Destroy after 3 years provided no legal action is pending.

series_title: Bureau of Workers' Compensation Claim Fo
series_description: Contains copies of Bureau of Workers' Compensation Forms that are with ODH employee accident form and used for occupational safety & health Administration (OSHA)
retention_statement: Paper: Retain for 5 years

series_description: The form contains information submitted by ODH to order supplies or services from the Consumer Product Safety Commission (U.S.C.P.S.C.) It is used by U.S.C.P.S.C. to document the purchase of service to be performed.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain 3 years

series_description: Contains information used to document the permits issued by a local health department for private water systems and to indicate the amount of fee returned to ODH.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain for 2 years

series_description: Contains information documenting discrimination cases and employee relations. Used by EEO officer & Civil Rights officer to track ODH compliance with State & Federal regulations.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain information for 3 years or after resoulution of a pending complaint

series_description: Records contain information on individuals participating in Ohio HIV counselling and testing program.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain for 10 years after of hard copy ; Paper: Retain for 2 years

series_description: Contains records tracking compliance/non-comp- liance in hiring/promotion for AFSCME #1199 Employees. This information is used to track employee grievances.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain for 5 years

series_description: Contains data on individuals related to their reported risk behaviors. Includes personal and medical information.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain on diskette for 1 year

series_description: Periodically submitted reports/tables documenting the racial-ethnic breakdown of employees in programs and projects receiving funds from govermental sources. May include or consist of EEO Reports and/or Affirmative Action Reports.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain for the same period as the records for the program, grant, project, etc., to which they pertain.

series_description: Survey of hospital in-house policies regarding staff and patient immunization levels, patient health education on immunization, infection/transmission control measures, etc.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain 5 years after survey is completed and analysis and reports are compiled ; Paper: Retain 3 months after data entry is completed and it accuracy is verified

series_description: Invoices for income owed to the department for services provided.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain four years

series_description: Hearing Aid Dealers and Fitters Licensing Board Application for License and application for Renewal of Trainee Permit contain- ing personal and health status data on the applicants.
retention_statement: Paper: Maintain in office until superseded by a new application or the applicant's business enterprise has been closed for 2 years.

series_description: Provides monthly and cumulative statistical information about syphilis and gonorrhea morbidity by county.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain for 5 years

series_title: APPLICATION FOR BIRTH OR DEATH CERTIFICATE
series_description: General public requests for certified certificates for births, still births, and death.
retention_statement: Paper: Two (2) years or until annual audit by State Auditor's Office, whichever is shorter.

series_title: GAF-26 Cash in Treasury Detail Ledger
series_description: Cash in Treasury Detail Ledger
retention_statement: Paper: Retain on year in office area

series_description: Forms used to indicate receipt of goods and/or services.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain in office area for one year. Transfer to the State Records Center for two additional years. Destroy after audited by the Auditor of State and the audit report is released.

series_description: Includes informaiton about patient's name, local health department, type of lab, type of tests and results, regarding suspected food poisoning cases.
retention_statement: Paper: Maintain in office for one year

series_title: Hospitals and Home Health Agency Survey
series_description: Contains records generated by hospitals and home health agencies in the survey certification process. The information also serves to document compliance/noncompliance with state and federal regulations
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain for 3 years after the hard copy is destroyed ; Paper: Retain for 3 years after the year in which the record was prepared

series_description: Includes any provider/facility complaint forms such as Complaint Unit Intake Form, Medicare/Medicaid Complaint Form, ODH Summary of Complaint Form, and Long Term Care Quality Assurance Forms. May contain confidential complaint or patient information.
retention_statement: Paper: Destroy unsubstantiated complaints after 1 year. Destroy substantiated complaints after 3 years.

series_description: Contains survey information on the health of individuals. Includes current and past health status, name, age, sex, address, race, date of birth, and behavioral and disease status. Consists of questionnaires, reproductive outcomes, homicide and miscellan
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain for 25 years ; Paper: Retain for 25 years

series_description: Contains information monitoring ODH's affirmative action efforts in order to detect and correct any problem areas in the employ- ment process.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain for 1 year and after audited

series_description: Contains accident or illness report (ADM 4303) and Employer Report (ADM 4303) for ODH employees.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain for 5 years

series_title: Application for Registration as a Privat
series_description: Contains information on individual registering as a private water systems contractor (name, address, and etc.). The original application is the only signed registration.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain for 3 years after last registration

series_description: Contains blood pressure information and referral forms for State of Ohio employees.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain for 2 years after termination of employment

series_description: Contains information gathered for individual cases of childhood lead poisoning.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain for 2 years after of hard copy ; Paper: Retain for 5 years

series_description: This record tracks the sexually transmitted disease morbidity and sorts the number of cases by source of infection, race and sex. This report does not contain the names of any infected individuals.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain for 10 years

series_description: Provides information about indigent people who are unable to reimburse medical providers for medical care related to a motor vehicle accident and their eligibility to have the Hospital Motor Vehicle Claims program reimburse that medical provider for relat
retention_statement: Paper: Retain in office for 2 years, after payment, transfer to State Record Center for 5 years

series_title: Radon Mitigation Inspection and Records Inspection
series_description: Inspection reports of records and mitigation and identifying system, location and contractor name documenting compliance/non-compliance with radon, rules, laws, protocols and procedures, test locations and results.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain 2 years after expiration of license in office

series_description: The purpose of this file is to maintain a record of drugs which have been purchased for distribution to local health departments, private physicians, clinics and hospitals. The file shows drugs ordered, lot number, expiration date, quanity, costs, program and activity code.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain in office area current fiscal year and two previous fiscal years

series_description: The purpose of this file is to keep a record of drugs sent to clinics, hospitals, and local health departments. This record contains receipts showing type of drug lot number, amount ordered, and the clinic, hospital, or local health department where received.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain in office area current fiscal year and two previous fiscal years

series_description: The purpose of this file is to show who ordered the item, quanity ordered, quanity received. This file consists of work requests and receiving reports of all literature and forms.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain in office area current fiscal year and two previous fiscal years

series_description: Fee cards and check list are used to calculate fee payments to local registrars for processing birth, death certificates and Probate Court who process the marriage and divorce abstracts.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain two years

series_description: Contains paternity, adoption, and related sealed, non-public legal documents, and court ordered corrections to records. The documents are contained in envelopes.
retention_statement: Paper: Permanent retention.

series_description: The indexes provide location for delayed registrations, corrections and related court actions; including birth, death, still-birth, marriages, and divorces.
retention_statement: Microform: Permanen ; Paper: Permanen

series_description: Meeting files including agenda, minutes, correspondence and related documents.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain for 5 years ; Electronic: Retain for 5 years

series_description: Includes informaiton about the serotype for salmonella and other miscellaneous bacteria. Information is communicated through lab slips, work cards, log sheets, and include information about patient's name, date of specimen collection, source of specimen
retention_statement: Paper: Retain in office for six months

series_description: Includes informaiton from "personal log books", "lab slips", customer names, phone numbers, site location, posted results of testing, and the type test performed, received from the EPA, ODH/RAD Health, and private sources.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain in office for one year

series_title: Abuse and Neglect Investigation Case Fil
series_description: The file contains information gathered in abuse and neglect investigations and the index cards are used for cross referencing the files.
retention_statement: Paper: Expunge files of unsubstantiated cases and destroy all accumulated cases on a quarterly basis. Substantiated cases will be retained 10 years

series_description: Contains the following nurse aide information: registry and employment verification out-of-state reciprocity request forms. This information applies to individual nurse aides.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain for 3 years after the hard copy is destroyed ; Paper: Retain for 2 years

series_description: Surveys inquire about health conditions, environment, patients or individuals. Surveys include demographic data of the respondent/subject, and frequently include patient identifiers.
retention_statement: Paper: Maintain for 1 year

series_description: Includes the following records: Report/Census of ailments of afflictions and the daily employee visit list and weight sheet.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain for 2 years

series_description: Contains information used to monitor and verify the operations of the HMO.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain for one survey period

series_title: Basic Life Support Community Training Ce
series_description: Contains information which promotes and teaches Basic Life Support/ C.P.R. to Odh employees and the community. Includes list of particpants and participant evaluations.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain for 2 years after of hard copy ; Paper: Retain for 3 1/2 years

series_description: Contains diagrams and drawings of agriculture labor camps such as diagrams of unit placement and actual blueprints of bulidings contained on the labor camp. Plans and diagrams are used for evaluating compliance with state laws and regulations.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain for the life of the labor camp. Review by State Archives prior to .

series_description: Documentaion backup of the findings of a federally funded Medicare Influenza Demonstration project (one of 10 projects conducted nationwide). Includes provider files, lab reports, case surveillance reports, grant reports, etc.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain 5 years after project is completed and analyses and reports are compiled. Review by State Archives

series_description: These files record the registration and inspection status of various facilities or persons concerning their compliance or noncompliance with state and federal radiation safety regulations. Files contain such documents as: applications, admendments, insp
retention_statement: Paper: Retain registration/insepction files for three years. Destroy provided no legal action pending.

series_description: Includes information about the serotyping of specimens for salmonella and other miscellaneous bacteria. Includes inform- ation from lab reports, data cards, and logs. Laboratory reports include informaiton on patient name, requesting physician, date of
retention_statement: Paper: Retain in office area for six month

series_description: Files include information about contractor's application which contains social security numbers, license to operate, quality assurance and control procedures, radiological safety plans, violations, and related correspondence.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain 2 years in office after expiration of license. Transfer to Records Retention for 3 additional years.

series_description: Correspondence, assignments and policies regarding state owned motor vehicle received from other agencies or by the Ohio Department of Health.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain in office until updated or superceded

series_title: Report of Positive Laboratory Findings for Class "A" Diseases
series_description: Report documents positive and negative laboratory test results for various sexually transmitted diseases. Information is for statistical analysis
retention_statement: Paper: Retain until statistics are tabulated

series_description: Report documents positive laboratory findings, epidemiological cases, contracts, suspects, associates as well as patient identification and location. A) in-state contacts, associates, and suspects (see retention period) B) out-of-state contacts, associates and suspects (same as STD-33-04 (a)) C) Laboratory Positives (same as STD 33-04(a)). After "central search" search, either new-entry or up-date is entered into registry.
retention_statement: Paper: A) Retain for 1 year

series_description: Includes information about gonorrhea and related miscellaneous organisms specimen results. Also includes name of the patient, provider, city, and the tests results. Also includes specimen results for- warded to the centers for disease control for verifi
retention_statement: Paper: Maintain in office for six months

series_description: Applications withdrawn by the applicant prior to final SHPDA Decision.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain 60 days

series_description: Informal, evidentiary, and licensure hearings on proposed revocation of a nursing home's certification or license. Case histories of illegal nursing homes included.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain in office 10 years. Review for Archival content or involvement in current litigation; if no historic value or litigation, destroy. (Archival review conducted by State )

series_description: Original source volumes used in look up of earlier records of birth, death, stillbirth (fetal Birth), marriage and divorce abstract. Certified copies are issued from the actual record once the volume and certificate number are obtained from these index v
retention_statement: Paper: Retain in office until end of calendar year

series_title: Births, Deaths, and Stillbirth Certificates and Marriage and Divorce Abstracts
series_description: Original birth certificate, death certificate, stillbirth certificate, marriage abstracts, and divorce abstract, and delayed birth certificates. These certificates are in books of 250 each on open shelve, which have to be accessible.
retention_statement: Paper: These records are retained as permanent documents within the Division of Vital Statisitics. Death records which are more than 50 years old are in the State at the Ohio Historical Center

series_description: Contains information on laboratories applying for BAAC certification. The application is the only record of laboratory certifi- cation. BAAC = Bureau of Acute Ancillary Care
retention_statement: Paper: Destroy 5 years after the closing of the facility, provided no legal action is pending.

series_description: Provides death and disease statistics on a City, County, and State basis of current and projected trends.
retention_statement: Electronic: Maintain in office for 1 year, or until it is superseded ; Paper: Maintain in office for 1 year, or until it is superseded

series_title: Out of State Births, Deaths, & Fetal Dea
series_description: Copies of out of state births, deaths, and fetal deaths received from other states in the United States for purposes of extracting statistical data sa per the inter-state transcript agreement between states.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain one year

series_description: This record contains cards used to gather data on individuals attending the food protection certification course. This card is not renewed and is continually in force as the only record of certification.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain for 5 years

series_description: Contains information that documents the licensure of hospices that are not surveyed by long term care.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain 6 years in office

series_description: Records demographic information about people who participate in Department of Health counseling programs that were tested for the HIV virus (AIDS). Includes information such as age, race, sex, locality, & other related information. (Contains no names or
retention_statement: Electronic: Maintain data in hard drive & on floppy disks for 3 years; maintain floppy disks at an approved records center & maintain hard drive in office area. Destroy hard drive & floppies provided a final report is completed ; Paper: Maintain in the office area for 3 years

series_description: Includes child and family health services biennial site report, confirmation letter and follow-up letter. To review compliance with program regulations.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain for 2 years ; Paper: Retain for 2 years

series_description: Used to request data entry services from Data Services. Serves as documentation of requests and information is entered on to a Request Log.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain until job is completed

series_description: Contains a computer generated list of licensed Agricultural Labor Camps.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain until audited ; Paper: Retain until audited

series_description: Record of voluntary registration of local cholesterol screening programs. Includes information about the applicant, program staff and program evaluation.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain for 2 years

series_description: Contains completed plan for application of dental sealants in school-based sealant programs. Instructs dental hygienists on need for dental sealants.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain 2 years

series_description: Actions/Responses by Legal Staff to proposed, pending or final state legislation affecting the Health Department and it various bureaus/divisions/programs.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain in office 5 years. Contact State for potential archival value, if none

series_description: Provides summary informaiton about activity per- formed by lab personnel. Information includes number of samples tested and demorgaphic and geogrkaphic data is well as other related information.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain in office for five years

series_description: Includes information about clients/businesses who request testing and/or lab services. Information is maintained on a monthly basis, lists the requesting parties, the test/service, and any other related information related to the requesting party.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain in office for one year

series_description: Includes information about specimens/samples that are collected for the purpose of presenting evidence to court. The evidence may be collected from government, private or corporate sources; included in the reports are who the lab techs were that tested
retention_statement: Paper: Retain in office five years after the close of the case file

series_description: Provides information about the ability of labora- tory personnel to produce specimen testing results consisten with other certified laboratories. Includes information about the samples, results of tests, retests, and correspondence relating to the produc
retention_statement: Paper: Retain for two years in the office

series_description: Provides information about serology and viral iso- lation test results from blood serum samples (example = FTA, HIV, Etc.).
retention_statement: Electronic: Maintain file for two years, destroy file in conjuction with of paper files ; Paper: Maintain in office for one year

series_description: Infant High Risk Questionnaire & Infant Hearing Assessment Reporting Form. In re: State Infant Hearing Screening & Assess- ment Program. Information gathered & maintained for purpose of data analysis on retrospective, as well as prospective longitudina
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain 2 years ; Paper: Maintain questionnaire and Hearing Assessment Reporting form until information is tabulated/entered

series_description: Provides information about asbestos removal projects that give notice to the Department of Health about asbestos abatement jobs, identification of the contractor, job site, start date, the specialist, the amount of asbestos to be removed, the type of remo
retention_statement: Paper: Maintain for 3 years

series_title: Ryan White CARE Program Intake Form
series_description: Contains Client Data Intake Report which is completed by every patient participating in the Ryan White CARE Program. Includes a patient ID number, address, insurance information, physican information and other demographic information.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain for 10 years after of hard copy ; Paper: Retain for 10 years after destruction of hard copy

series_description: File for each registered X-ray machine or radiation source in Ohio contains application, FDA certificate, field test and inspection records. All files for a facility are maintained togather in a facility file.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain radiation source file for the life of the source plus 2 inspection periods

series_description: Contains files including newborn screening results, physicians reports, and correspondence. Information documents the newborn screening tests and lab work performed as well as confirmation that formula was shipped to the patient.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain for duration of particip- ation in the program.

series_title: Report of Civilian Cases of STD's by Sex
series_description: Contains reports on the number of sexually transmitted diseases (STD) reported to the Ohio Department of Health by age, sex and race. The information does not contain the names of individuals. The reports are compiled into reports that are generated by
retention_statement: Paper: Retain for 20 years

series_description: This report contains the number of positive HIV reports received for each county in Ohio per month. This record does not contain the names of the infected individuals. This information is reported to ODH so that ODH can track the incidences of HIV and g
retention_statement: Paper: Retain for 10 years

series_title: Faxes Form the Solid Transplant Consortium
series_description: Faxes from the Solid Transplant Consortium are received by staff physicians for their review and consideration. Faxes include patient information and transplant options. Decisions and recommendations are telephoned back to the Consortium.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain faxes for 3 months in the Director's office, and 1 year and 9 months in storage

series_description: Includes all records which document the licensing of labs to perform breathe analyzer testing. Includes testing results and various licensing information (i.e., applications) revocations.
retention_statement: Microform: Maintain for five years ; Paper: Retain in office for five years

series_description: Specific observation details noted by lab staff about the test specimen's reactions during testing.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain in office area for one year

series_description: Includes informaiton about vectors such as insects ticks, mammals, and birds, which transmit diseases to humans. Includes testing of these disease hosts, and serology testing. Included diseases are lyme disease, rocky mountain spotted, arvo virus, and en
retention_statement: Paper: Maintain in office for two years

series_description: Report document new cases in "STD central registry"
retention_statement: Paper: Retain until data is entered into registry

series_title: Ohio Venereal Disease Register Card (1238-01D and 1238-05D)
series_description: Report maintain information on sysphilis cases (confirmed cases)
retention_statement: Paper: Retain until patient is: A) 50 years or older without up-date in 5 years. B) 40-49 years or older without update in 10 years. C) no age without up-date in 10 years. D) entered as "no name"

series_description: Files related to resident discharge or transfer hearings, including, but not limited to, correspondence, exhibits, decisions, hearing recordings.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain 3 years after decision ; Electronic: Retain 3 years after decision

series_description: Contains applications, Hospital and Home Report Forms, Statistical Report Forms, Correspondence, Architectural Plans, Perintal Regional Correspondence, Survey Reports, and Licensure Status of Maternity Hospitals and Maternity Home Units.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain for 5 years in office

series_description: Provides information regarding those who receive and handled lab specimens.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain in office for five years

series_description: Includes information regarding the proficiency of other laboratories to test controlled specimens correctly.
retention_statement: Paper: Maintain in office area for one year

series_description: Includes informaiton about the type of lab kit for supplies requested. Listed onthe form is the requesting party, the available kits offered, the requestors address, and the date, the lab responded to the request.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain in office area for one year

series_description: Contains abuse and neglect logs which contain chronological information about each complaint.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain for 1 year

series_description: Contains medical information gathered from ODH employees about blood pressure, weight and family history.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain for 3 years after of hard copy ; Paper: Retain for 2 years

series_description: Contains documentation of employee health service activities reported on a bi-weekly basis.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain for 2 years ; Paper: Retain for 1 year

series_title: Application for Assistance to Adjust Flo
series_description: Contains information regarding water supplies and applicants for fluoride grant funds.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain for 3 years

series_title: Survey of High Risk Clients Regarding AI
series_description: These records surveys targeted populations to determine their understanding of & behavior to AIDS. Surveys include information about population's age, race, sex, sexual preferences, socio- economic status, marital status, & behavioral/social value-relate
retention_statement: Electronic: Maintain on hard drive in office and maintain floppy disk in an approved records storage center for two years; ; Paper: Maintain in office for 2 years

series_description: Contains complaints received by ODH in regards to Health Maintenance organizations. Includes name of person filling complaint.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain unsubstantiated complaints for 1 year

series_description: Charts maintained on each individual receiving services at LHC or by refferal from LHC contains patient identifiers and demographic data as well as information on services provided, diagnosois/ medcial conditions found, laboratory results etc..
retention_statement: Paper: Maintain 3 years in office

series_description: Contains an order form sent to ODH to order educational materials such as pamphlets, stickers, bookmarkers and posters. Includes payment and order form.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain for 2 years ; Paper: Retain for 2 years

series_description: Cases of: (1) Unfounded complaints dismissed without hearing (2) Cases unsubstantiated in hearing (3) Substantiated complaints against staff of Long Term Care Facilities
retention_statement: Paper: Expunge unfounded and unsubstan- tiated complaint files and destroy on a quarterly basis. Retain substantiated cases 10 years

series_description: This record compiles information to compare the present number of sexually transmitted diseases in major cities to the number of incidences the previous year.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain for 10 years

series_description: Completed participants' course evaluation questionaires )rating course subject matter, trainer effectiveness, organization and environment, etc of training course), tally sheets, and statistical summaries of any training courses conducted by the departmen
retention_statement: Paper: Retain raw data sheets 1 year

series_description: Contains application, training certification, continuing education information, radiological safety plan, quality assurance and quality control procedures, licensing correspondence and personal information including social security numbers of applicants.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain 2 years after expiration of license in office

series_description: This record series pertains to Medicare certification applications submitted by prospective providers/suppliers of the Medicare program (ie. Skilled nursing facilities, home health agencies, ambulatory surgical centers, etc.) but did not result in Medicare certification (ie. Withdrawl of application, failure to produce complete application by specified timeframe).
retention_statement: Paper: Nonparticipating Facilities: retain materials for one year following the year in which the last certification contact was made. This schedule pertains only to applications that did not result in Medicare certification (ie., withdrawal of application, incomplete application whereby applicant failed to produce requested forms and paperwork by specified deadline)

series_description: Agencies use this form to summarize all out-of- state travel done by personnel for each quarter.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain one year in the office

series_description: Payroll Warrant Cancellation Advise & Transmittal. Used to cancel a payroll warrant issued by an agency in error.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain in office one year, transfer to State Records Center for three years. Destroy provided audited by Auditor of State and audit report is released.

series_description: Provides information regarding the certification status of various health care facilities. Included are applications to become a provider, disclosure of ownership and control interest statements, certification & transmittal forms, statements of deficienc
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain exit interview tapes for 5 years ; Paper: Retain in office for 3 years after the year in which the record was prepared. Store for 2 years offsite

series_description: Contains unsuccessful applications (in-state and out-of state) for licensure as a nursing home administrator. Includes personal information about the applicant such as name, education, employment, criminal and medical records.
retention_statement: Paper: Destroy after 2 years.

series_title: Application for Registration as a Provid
series_description: Applications by colleges, governmental agencies, and state wide associations to be registered as providers of continuing education. The application is in force continually until either the provider of continuing education or ODH decide to end the relatio
retention_statement: Paper: Retain 1 year after relationship has been severed.

series_description: Contains information documenting that there is a 15% minority participation in the purchasing of services and products.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain 3 years

series_description: Contains Ohio Public Employee Occupational Safety and Health Program annual report survey form for the Departments of State as required by Executive Order 83-62.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain for 3 years ; Paper: Retain for 3 years

series_description: Contains summary reports of HIV counselling and testing programs in Ohio.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain for 10 years

series_description: Record is used to log and track all incoming Data Services requests received from all ODH Divisions.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain for 1 year

series_description: Contains reports of occupational diseases submitted by physicians pursuant to Section 3701.25 of the Ohio Revised Code.
retention_statement: Electronic: Destroy 5 years after of hard copy ; Paper: Retain for 5 years

series_description: This record is used by field staff during field investigations of clients with sexually transmitted diseases. The record contains personal information on the infected individual (Name, address, etc.) and also tracks partners (in and out of the state of O
retention_statement: Paper: Retain for 1 year

series_description: Contains information regarding medical providers which are participating in Bureau of Children with Medical Handicaps program including communication between the provider and the BCMH program.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain until provider is inactive for 3 years;

series_description: Contain court ordered name changes.
retention_statement: Paper: Permanent record pursuant to Chapter 3705 of the Ohio Revised Code.

series_title: Certificate of Registration of Nursing H
series_description: State of Ohio NCR copy of the Certificate of Registration to practice as a Nursing Home Administrator, containing license number, registration number, and certificate expiration date.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain 1 year after expiration

series_description: Includes, but not limited to, Complaint Intake Form (HEA 1681), Report of Investigation of Complaint (HEA 1602), ODH Complaint QA Form, and various correspondence and supporting documentation. May contain confidential complaint or patient information.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain for a total of 5 years; three in office

series_description: This series includes the drug analysis application submitted to the Ohio Dept. of Health''s Bureau of Alcohol and Drug Testing. Included on the application is: the applicant''s name, the name of the laboratory where they are employed, telephone number of the facility, position the applicant is applying for, name of national proficiency testing program, applicable education and degrees, the applications''s signature and date. The director of the laboratory, where the individual will be working, must relate that the laboratory has a current and an updated manual and internal policy which is posted in the area where the individual will be conducting the drug analysis work, in compliance with 3701-53-06 and he also must verify the quality of the lab, the qualifications and certification of the applicant and the director, and that the director will be responsible to monitor the work and quality of work completed by the applicant.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain the application paperwork in the Bureau for two years, and

series_description: This series includes certificates authorizing performance of breath tests, certificate authorizing performance of laboratory tests using alcohol method for enzymatic, an application for laboratory permit for alcohol analysis and a breath alcohol permit application. These documents confirm application and certification of performance of breath testing and alcohol analysis.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain the application and copies of certificates in Bureau of Alcohol and Drug Testing for two years

series_description: This series includes the Alco-Sensor RBT III Instrument Checklist, the Alco-Sensor RBT III Operational Checklist, the Intoxilyzer 5000 Instrument Checklist, the Intoxilyzer 5000 Operational Checklist, the BAC Datamaster Instrument Checklist, the BAC Operational Checklist, the BAC Verifier Instrument Checklist and the BAC Verifier Opeational Checklist. These records are verification that the instrument used to do the test is in proper working order, the test results are accurate and to identify the subject being given the test.
retention_statement: Paper: The records are to be maintained for three years following the date of decertification of the instrument.

series_description: This series includes the Alco-Sensor RBT III Study Guide, the BAC DataMaster Basic Training Manual, the BAC DataMaster Renewal Study Guide, the BAC DataMaster Study Review Questions, the BAC DataMaster Training Checklist, the BAC Verifier Renewal Study Guide, the BAC Verifier Study Review Questions, the BAC Verifier Training Checklist, the Intoxilyzer 5000 Basic Training Manual, the Intoxilyzer 5000 Renewal Study Guide, the Intoxilyzer 5000 Study Review Questions and the Intoxilyzer 5000 Training Checklist. These records are used in the training of the operation of the alcohol and drug testing equipment used by the Bureau of Alcohol and Drug Testing.
retention_statement: Paper: The training information to be retained for three years following decertification date of the equipment.

series_description: This series includes the Exam Booklet Basic Training, Exam Booklet In-Service Training, Scantron Alcohol Breath Testing Application/Examination, training certification and training evaluation form. These forms verify the training and test results of alcohol/drug testing applicants.
retention_statement: Paper: These records to be maintained by the Bureau of Alcohol and Drug Testing for three years following decertification of applicant.

series_description: Records include, but not limited to: patient identifiers, medical exam findings, laboratory test results, immunization records, and sometimes case notes.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain 5 years after case closed

series_description: This record series contains documents and forms related to the application and subsequent approval/disapproval to operate a nurse aide training program. Documentation includes, but is not limited to; the application, couse agenda/criteria, trainer qualifications, approval/disapproval letters, class schedule, trainee report, revocation and other ''enforcement'' letters and general correspondence.
retention_statement: Paper: Active Programs: Retain records for five years after the year in which the record was prepared. Destroy after five year period provided that no legal action is pending against the facility and all appeals have been resolved. Inactive or Closed Programs: Retain records two additional years following the year in which final action was taken with respect to denial, termination, or withdrawal. Destroy record after this two year period provided that no legal action is pending against the facility and all appeals have been resolved ; Electronic: Same retention period as paper

series_description: Ohio Department of Health''''''''s copy of subgrantees single, and independant (financial report) audits.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain 5 years after audit is completed

series_description: Documents pertaining to public records requests, including, but not limited to, the original request, correspondence between staff and requestor, redacted and un-redacted responsive records. Maintained to document compliance with public records laws. Third-party subpoenas for agency documents, correspondence and responsive records.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain 1 year, unless pending litigation. ; Electronic: Retain 1 year, unless pending litigation.

series_description: Files of meetings of the Public Health Council, including, but not limited to, agendas, meeting minutes, correspondence, other meeting related records.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain 5 years ; Electronic: Retain 5 years

series_title: Written Contracts for goods or services and Competitive Bids
series_description: Written contracts for goods, services, grants, sub-grants, temporary personnel, purchase orders and all related documentation including requests for proposals, competitive bids, responses, evaluations, clarifications, awards, protest, change orders, amendments and performance bond documents.
retention_statement: Paper: For written contracts and related RFP documents terminating prior to June 16, 2021, retain 8 years after contract termination ; Electronic: or written contracts and related RFP documents which terminated prior to June 16, 2021, retain 8 years after contract termination

series_description: Pregnancy-Associated Mortality Review (PAMR) records. Records of any services to women who died during pregnancy or within one year of pregnancy. These records can include: Healthcare Records: Admission history for all hospital admissions, Autopsy Report, Physical examination records from all hospital admissions, Coroner’s Notes, Declaration of Death, Discharge summary for all hospital admissions, Emergency Medical Services (EMS) Transport Records, Labor and Delivery Records, Prenatal Care Records, Postpartum Care Records, Social Service Records, Mental Health Records, Substance Use Disorder Records, Plan of Safe Care, Operative and Procedure Reports, Nursing Notes, Physician Progress Notes, Laboratory Test Results, Anesthesia Records, Consultation and Referral Records, Vitals Flow Sheets, Toxicology Report, Investigator’s Notes, Legal Records, Other: Complete Records Requested for care two years preceding death. Police Reports: 911-Detail Reports, MVA Crash Reports, Computer Aided Dispatch (CAD)-Reports, Motor Vehicle Accidents (MVA) Forensic Reports, Incident Reports, Arrest Reports, Incident Reports, Investigative Reports, Missing Person Reports and/or "be on the look out" (BOLO) Reports.
retention_statement: Paper: The PAMR Committee annually reviews pregnancy-associated deaths. The records they review will be disposed of 7 years after the completion of PAMR committee review. ; Electronic: The PAMR Committee annually reviews pregnancy-associated deaths. The records they review will be disposed of 7 years after the completion of PAMR committee review.

series_description: State WIC and projects maintain WIC records so that auditors, state and federal evaluators, and participants can have access to them when necessary. Records include, but are not limited to, information pertaining to financial operations, food delivery systems, food instrument issuance and redemption, equipment purchases and inventory, certification, nutrition education, including breastfeeding promotion and support, civil rights and fair hearing procedures.
retention_statement: Paper: All physical documentation shall be kept for three years and nine months past the submission of the final expenditure report for which the item pertains. If any litigation, claim, negotiation, audit or other action involving the records has been started before the end of the three-year and nine month period, the records shall be kept until all issues are resolved, or until the end of the regular three-year period, whichever is later. ; Electronic: All electronic documentation shall be kept for three years and nine months past the submission of the final expenditure report for which the item pertains. If any litigation, claim, negotiation, audit or other action involving the records has been started before the end of the three-year and nine month period, the records shall be kept until all issues are resolved, or until the end of the regular three-year period, whichever is later.

series_title: Bureau of Infectious Diseases: Animal Bite Reports
series_description: This series includes reports on the number of reported animal bites. Animal bites must be report to the Ohio Department of Health by local health departments under 3701-3-28(B).
retention_statement: Paper: Retain in office 5 years. Review for Archival content; if no historical value, destroy. Archival review conducted by State . ; Electronic: Retain in office 5 years. Review for Archival content; if no historical value, destroy. Archival review conducted by State Archives.

series_title: Bureau of Infectious Diseases: Outbreak Line Lists
series_description: Line lists created for administrative purposes during active public health investigations of outbreaks reported to the Ohio Department of Health's Bureau of Infectious Diseases under 3701-3-02(C).
retention_statement: Paper: Data in the electronic database ODRS is retained until superseded. Hardcopy/paper and Electronic documents- Retain until no longer of administrative value (e.g., outbreak has been closed and any applicable outbreak report is completed) ; Electronic: Data in the electronic database ODRS is retained until superseded. Hardcopy/paper and Electronic documents- Retain until no longer of administrative value (e.g., outbreak has been closed and any applicable outbreak report is completed)

series_title: Bureau of Infectious Diseases: Annual Reports of Infectious Diseases
series_description: Reports produced by the Ohio Department of Health on distribution of reportable infectious diseases including demographic and geographic breakdowns.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain in office 20 years. Review for Archival content; if no historical value, destroy. Archival review conducted by State . ; Electronic: Retain in office 20 years. Review for Archival content; if no historical value, destroy. Archival review conducted by State Archives.

series_title: Bureau of Infectious Diseases: Outbreak Reports
series_description: This series includes outbreak reports received by the Ohio Department of Health's Bureau of Infectious Diseases under Ohio Administrative Code 3701-3-02(C).
retention_statement: Paper: Data in the electronic database ODRS is retained until superseded. Hardcopy/paper and Electronic documents- destroy 6 months after entering information into the database. Destroy outbreak records that pre-date electronic database. ; Electronic: Data in the electronic database ODRS is retained until superseded. Hardcopy/paper and Electronic documents- destroy 6 months after entering information into the database. Destroy outbreak records that pre-date electronic database.

series_title: Bureau of Infectious Diseases: Individual Disease Reports
series_description: This series includes individual disease reports received by the Ohio Department of Health's Bureau of Infectious Diseases under Ohio Administrative Code 3701-3-02(A) and 3701-3-02(B). Information included in reports is described in OAC 3701-3-03 and 3701-3-04. Reports are entered into the electronic Ohio Disease Reporting System (ODRS) for appropriate public health surveillance follow-up. Reports of disease that are not specified in 3701-3-02 (e.g., "non-reportable" conditions) should be destroyed upon review, unless the file is covered under a separate schedule.
retention_statement: Paper: Data in the electronic database ODRS is retained until superseded. Hardcopy/paper and Electronic documents- destroy 6 months after entering information into the database. Destroy individual records that pre-date electronic database. ; Electronic: Data in the electronic database ODRS is retained until superseded. Hardcopy/paper and Electronic documents- destroy 6 months after entering information into the database. Destroy individual records that pre-date electronic database.

series_description: This series includes mosquito and tick surveillance and testing data collected by the Ohio Department of Health or collected and reported by local health districts. Information can include location and dates of trapping/surveillance, vector species identified, and testing status for pathogens.
retention_statement: Paper: Summary electronic records (e.g., Excel database)- retain until superseded. Hardcopy/paper and Electronic documents- Retain for 2 years ; Electronic: Summary electronic records (e.g., Excel database)- retain until superseded. Hardcopy/paper and Electronic documents- Retain for 2 years
